description: Workspace: `ee/packages/calendar` exists with valid package metadata.
Workspace: `ee/packages/microsoft-teams` exists with valid package metadata.
Workspace: new EE package names do not collide with existing `packages/teams` or other workspace packages.
Exports: enterprise calendar root index resolves without deep path imports.
Exports: enterprise Microsoft Teams root index resolves without deep path imports.
Exports: enterprise calendar actions entrypoint resolves for shared callers.
Exports: enterprise Microsoft Teams actions entrypoint resolves for shared callers.
Exports: enterprise calendar components entrypoint resolves for settings or profile wrappers.
Exports: enterprise Microsoft Teams components entrypoint resolves for settings wrappers.
TypeScript: enterprise calendar package tsconfig builds local source without unresolved imports.
TypeScript: enterprise Microsoft Teams package tsconfig builds local source without unresolved imports.
Dependencies: React or peer dependency declarations are valid for both new EE packages.
Ownership: enterprise calendar actions are imported from the calendar package rather than `packages/ee`.
Ownership: enterprise calendar integrations settings UI is imported from the calendar package rather than `packages/ee`.
Ownership: enterprise calendar profile settings UI is imported from the calendar package rather than `packages/ee`.
Ownership: enterprise calendar provider services resolve from the calendar package.
Ownership: enterprise calendar sync service resolves from the calendar package.
Ownership: enterprise calendar webhook processor resolves from the calendar package.

Ownership: enterprise calendar provider adapters resolve from the calendar package.

Ownership: enterprise calendar job-handler logic resolves from the calendar package.

Route export: Google calendar webhook handler is exported by the calendar package.
Route export: Microsoft calendar webhook handler is exported by the calendar package.
Server handler API: calendar package exposes server-consumable route handler entrypoints with the expected exports.
UI API: calendar package exposes UI entrypoints consumable by EE and shared wrappers.
Deduplication: there is no remaining duplicate live calendar implementation split between `packages/ee` and `ee/server`.
Imports: enterprise calendar code does not deep-import `ee/server` implementation files.
Ownership: enterprise Teams actions are imported from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package rather than `packages/ee`.
Ownership: enterprise Teams package actions are imported from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package rather than `packages/ee`.

Ownership: enterprise Teams notification delivery resolves from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.

Ownership: enterprise Teams action registry resolves from the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Route export: Teams bot handlers are exported by the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.

Route export: Teams package-management handlers are exported by the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Route export: Teams quick-action handlers are exported by the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.
Page or server export: Teams tab handling entrypoints are exported by the enterprise Microsoft Teams package.

CI graph: enterprise Microsoft Teams package tasks are recognized with correct dependencies.

Cleanup: obsolete duplicate Teams files are removed or reduced after the migration.
